format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the # H1 title; no leading YAML. reading.meta.json exists, parses, and carries word_count/estimated_read_minutes/citations_used/sections. Exactly the six fixed sections, correct order.

length.notes: Word count and read-aloud minutes both inside the atomic profile budget under either wpm convention.

citations.notes: All three primary resources cited inline >=1 and present in the footnote-style References block; markers and bibliography match exactly.

forward_references.notes: Anchors referenced are all prior (5.4, 5.7, 5.10, 5.11). The In Practice softness-scale table LISTS the four frameworks with descriptive attributes (what/who/how-binding/how-fast) but teaches NO selection logic — no 'use X when', no 'which framework applies', no decision criteria. Selection-synthesis stays correctly deferred to 5.13. No reference to 5.13.

beginner_readability.notes: MEITY (L13), advisory (L17), intermediary/platform (L26), deepfake (L35), due diligence (L38) all defined in plain language on first use via bold-term shape. One idea at a time; concrete-first; plain register; no hedging stacks.

formatting.notes: Heavy bullet/numbered-list structure, bold-term definitions, comparison table, numbered March-2024 Worked Example. No paragraph exceeds ~4 sentences. Inline examples appear at concept introduction.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights in learner prose. Complies with gold decision 2026-06-11.

scope.notes: Stays on India MEITY advisories. IT Rules (2021), future tech legislation named-and-deferred only ('you do not need their detail' / 'not covered in this topic'); AIGG absent. Presented as evolving 2024 soft-law, not settled statute. No selection logic (reserved for 5.13).

sampled: MEITY = Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (corpus 5.1)
advisory is guidance not statute, can change fast (corpus 5.1)
two asks: label unreliable/AI output + due diligence (corpus 5.2)
transparency + harm prevention map to 5.4 pillars (corpus 5.2)
faithfulness.notes: Every sampled assertion traces verbatim or paraphrased to topic_corpus.md. No invented claims.

diagram_integrity.notes: embeds_diagram=false is correct; artifact_plan.diagram=false. No image references in reading.md, no broken refs. No script/meta artifacts leaked into learner prose.

verdict: PASS — reading 5.12 meets the atomic-profile reading-critic bar: clean format (no frontmatter, sidecar valid, six sections in order), 1010 words / ~5 min in range, all three primary refs cited and bibliographized, no forward references (softness-scale table lists the four frameworks but teaches no selection logic — correctly deferred to 5.13), MEITY/advisory/intermediary/deepfake/due-diligence all defined on first use, IT Rules 2021 and future legislation named-and-deferred, presented as evolving 2024 soft-law, fully faithful to corpus, no assessment prose, embeds_diagram=false correct. Meets the reading gold-example bar.
